title: Congenital Malformations of the Central Nervous System

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องรู้ CNS Malformations?
Brain malformations พบในเด็กทุก GA และเป็น significant morbidity + mortality ใน neonates. Key reasons: (1) Advances in imaging + genetics ทำให้ diagnose ได้ specific มากขึ้น — prognostication แม่นยำขึ้น; (2) Multidisciplinary team ต้องเริ่มตั้งแต่ NICU; (3) malformations หลายอย่าง mimic HIE (เช่น HPE, lissencephaly, 1p36 deletion) — ต้องแยก; (4) genetic testing เปลี่ยน management (recurrence risk, family planning); (5) Folate prevention ลด NTD 60–70% — Thailand มี fortification program; (6) Fetal surgery สำหรับ MMC เป็น standard of care ที่ selected centers.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย HPE spectrum (alobar/semilobar/lobar/MIH), genetics (SHH/ZIC2), clinical features, prognosis
รู้ ACC: prevalence, outcomes, associated conditions, prenatal screening approach
แยก SOD triad และ management (pituitary, vision monitoring)
อธิบาย microcephaly vs megalencephaly: causes, prognosis, mTOR pathway
เข้าใจ lissencephaly spectrum: LIS1, DCX, tubulin genes, cobblestone cortex dystroglycanopathies
รู้ PMG (polymicrogyria): bilateral perisylvian pattern, etiologies, clinical features
อธิบาย Dandy-Walker malformation, Joubert syndrome (molar tooth sign), PCH
รู้ Neural tube defects: epidemiology, folate prevention, anencephaly, MMC management
วางแผนการดูแล neonate กับ brain malformation: imaging, genetics, subspecialty referrals
1. Disorders of Prosencephalic Development
1.1 Holoprosencephaly (HPE)

HPE = most common brain malformation; live birth prevalence 1:10,000–1:20,000; แต่ 1:250 pregnancies (ส่วนใหญ่ miscarry early). 3 major cleavage planes (horizontal, sagittal, coronal) ที่ wk 3–4 of development. HPE = incomplete separation ตาม planes เหล่านี้.
1.1.1 Clinical Features
"The face predicts the brain": alobar HPE มักมี cyclopia, proboscis, cebocephaly; lobar/semilobar HPE บางรายมี normal facies แต่บางรายมี severe facial anomaly
Newborns: hypotonia, microcephaly (อาจ masked โดย hydrocephalus); DI ใน 70%; hypothyroidism, growth hormone deficiency; epilepsy 40% (1/3 intractable)
Survival: cytogenetically normal HPE > 50% alive at 12 months; with chromosome anomaly → neonatal death most
1.1.2 Genetics
Chromosomal 25–50%: trisomy 13, 18 most common; microarrays detect CNV in 10–20% additional
SHH mutations: most common (40% of familial HPE); SHH = sonic hedgehog signaling — cholesterol-dependent; statins → ↑ HPE risk
ZIC2: most common de novo mutation; high penetrance; characteristic face (bitemporal narrowing, upslant PF, large ears, short upturned nose)
Other genes: SIX3, TGIF, GLI2, PTCH1. Maternal diabetes → risk 1–2%
1.2 Agenesis of Corpus Callosum (ACC)
Corpus callosum = ~190 million axons; forms over ~11 weeks; development continues through adolescence
Prevalence: 0.5/10,000 general population → 600/10,000 in children with ND disabilities
Probst bundles: fiber bundles ที่ run parallel to ventricle (axons ที่ fail to cross midline); colpocephaly; eversion of cingulate gyri

Outcomes: systematic review → 75% developing typically at early school age; 11% severe disability. Associated microcephaly/epilepsy/cerebral palsy → worse outcome. Specific deficits: abstract reasoning, problem-solving, linguistic pragmatics
Workup: chromosome microarray; fetal MRI 20–22 weeks; ophthalmology, genetics, audiology, ND eval after birth
1.3 Septo-Optic Dysplasia (SOD)
Classic triad: (1) absent septum pellucidum, (2) optic nerve hypoplasia, (3) pituitary dysfunction. Need ≥ 2/3 features for diagnosis; 30% have all 3
Prevalence: 1:10,000 births; increased risk in younger mothers; mostly sporadic
Hypopituitarism in up to 80%: GH deficiency most common; evolves over time — lifelong monitoring needed. Risk: adrenal crisis, hypoglycemia
Management: brain MRI + ophthalmologic evaluation + pituitary function tests; endocrinology, neurology, development follow-up
2. Cortical Defects ใน Size and Organization
2.1 Microcephaly
OFC > 2 SD below mean; primary (evident at birth) vs secondary (progressive postnatal). ~60% ของ cases มี identifiable etiology (half genetic); 40% idiopathic
Genetic causes: aneuploidies, de novo CNVs, AR/AD/X-linked. Genes affect cell cycle, DNA repair, mitotic spindle. Secondary causes: Rett, Angelman (ASPM gene)
Zika virus (ZIKV): infects neural progenitor cells (NPCs); disrupts NPC differentiation + migration → severe microcephaly + congenital contractures; confirmed Flaviviridae family
Prognosis: severe (>3 SD): 80% DD + imaging anomalies; moderate (2–3 SD): much lower risk
2.2 Megalencephaly / Macrocephaly
PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway mutations: AKT3, PIK3R2, PIK3CA → megalencephaly syndromes; same pathway causes focal cortical dysplasia (FCD) and hemimegalencephaly when somatic/mosaic
Syndromes: MPPH (megalencephaly-polymicrogyria-polydactyly-hydrocephalus) + MCAP (megalencephaly-capillary malformation) — PIK3CA, PIK3R2
mTOR inhibitors (rapamycin/sirolimus): novel medical therapy สำหรับ non-surgical candidates
Hemimegalencephaly: dysplastic overgrowth ใน single hemisphere → hemispherectomy ใน neonatal period ถ้า severe epilepsy
3. Cortical Migration Defects
3.1 Lissencephaly — Classic Type 1 Spectrum

LIS1 mutations: most common; LIS1 = microtubule-associated protein (MAP) for nucleokinesis. Other genes: DCX (X-linked, males → lissencephaly, females → subcortical band heterotopia), YWHAE, tubulin genes (TUBA1A, TUBB2B, TUBB3)
Miller-Dieker syndrome: deletion LIS1 + YWHAE → severe lissencephaly + high forehead, bitemporal hallowing, short nose, upturned nares, small jaw. Head OFC normal at birth → progressive microcephaly
Clinical: hypotonia, DD, seizures (infantile spasms/Lennox-Gastaut ที่ 2nd half 1st year), feeding difficulties. X-linked ARX → lissencephaly + abnormal genitalia
RELN mutations: lissencephaly + severe global cerebellar hypoplasia + malformed hippocampus — Reelin encodes large ECM protein
3.2 Cobblestone Cortex (Type 2 Lissencephaly) — Dystroglycanopathies

rows: Severe cobblestone, kinked brainstem, cerebellar hypoplasia
Moderate cognitive disability, some walk independently

blocks.text: All autosomal recessive; all dystroglycanopathies — abnormal O-glycosylation of alpha-dystroglycan → disruption of pial surface → neurons overmigrate → cobblestone appearance
3.3 Periventricular Heterotopia (PVH)

FLNA mutations: bilateral contiguous PVH; clinical predominance in females (hemizygous FLNA lethal in males). FLNA = key cytoskeletal component for cell motility. 80% familial PVH; 20% sporadic. Associated cardiac problems → echo needed
ARFGEF2 mutations: PVH + microcephaly
Clinical: focal seizures; ND outcomes vary; may have learning disabilities
3.4 Subcortical Band Heterotopia
Band of heterotopic gray matter within white matter (DCX somatic mutations in males, germline in females). Severity correlates with band thickness. 25% have normal or near-normal intelligence
3.5 Polymicrogyria (PMG)
Irregular gray-white border + many small gyri or thickened cortical ribbon on MRI. Etiologies: genes, CMV, toxoplasmosis, ischemia, metabolic, vascular (COL4A1/COL4A2, 22q11 del, Sturge-Weber)
Bilateral perisylvian PMG: most common pattern → epilepsy + ID. Bilateral frontoparietal PMG → dysconjugate gaze/esotropia + DD + cerebellar signs (GPR56 mutations)
Clinical: depends on extent/location; bilateral or >50% of one hemisphere → moderate-severe DD + motor dysfunction; refractory epilepsy (onset may be postnatal)
4. Malformations of the Posterior Fossa
4.1 Dandy-Walker Malformation (DWM)
Triad: (1) vermis aplasia/hypoplasia, (2) cystic dilation of 4th ventricle, (3) enlargement of posterior fossa with ↑ torcula. Estimated prevalence ~1:5000 (likely lower with strict criteria)
Etiology: de novo variants, chromosomal (trisomy 9, 13, 18), multifactorial; recurrence 1–3%; exome sequencing found mutations in 16% DWM patients
Management: VP shunt ± posterior fossa shunt สำหรับ hydrocephalus; outcomes: isolated DWM มักมี mild ND issues; associated malformations → ↑ risk
4.2 Joubert Syndrome (JS) — Molar Tooth Sign

Joubert Syndrome — Molar Tooth Sign: สิ่งที่ต้องจำ
Molar tooth sign on axial MRI: (1) cerebellar vermis hypoplasia/aplasia; (2) deep interpeduncular fossa; (3) thick, elongated superior cerebellar peduncles. Clinical: hypotonia, abnormal eye movements (nystagmus, oculomotor apraxia), DD, apnea/tachypnea in infancy. Prevalence 1:100,000. Recurrence up to 25% (AR). Key monitoring: retinal dystrophy, fibrocystic kidneys, liver fibrosis — subsets by gene (CEP290 → retinal dystrophy). Pathogenic variants in >35 genes, most commonly AHI1, CC2D2A, CEP290, TMEM67, CPLANE1.
4.3 Pontocerebellar Hypoplasia (PCH)
Diverse AR disorders; variable pontine + cerebellar hypoplasia. Most common: TSEN54 mutations (PCH types 1, 2, 4, 5); up to 40% no identified cause
PCH Type 2: neonatal encephalopathy, progressive microcephaly, dyskinesia, seizures, cortical visual impairment; vermis less affected than hemispheres
Prognosis generally poor: severe DD, tone abnormalities, feeding issues, respiratory compromise
5. Chiari Malformations และ Neural Tube Defects
5.1 Chiari Malformations
Chiari I: inferior ectopia of cerebellar tonsils into spinal canal (>5–10mm + symptoms); syringomyelia/scoliosis; uncommonly neonatal presentation; may be congenital or acquired

Chiari II: almost exclusively with open NTDs (MMC); cerebellar vermis + medulla + 4th ventricle herniate into spinal canal; small posterior fossa; "beaked" tectum; hydrocephalus frequent; 60–90% develop hydrocephalus
Chiari III: occipital/high cervical encephalocele + kinked brainstem/spinal cord; limited life span, substantial disabilities
5.2 Neural Tube Defects — Overview

NTD Prevention — ข้อมูลที่ neonatologist ต้องแม่น
Incidence: 0.5–2:1000 pregnancies worldwide. Folate periconceptional → prevents 60–70% of NTDs. Most countries recommend healthy diet + folate supplement before conception through week 12. AFP screening optimal 16–18 weeks maternal serum. Ultrasound: "lemon sign" (frontal bossing) + "banana sign" (cerebellar) = sensitive for open NTD. Environmental risks: valproate, carbamazepine, maternal hyperthermia/hyperglycemia, fumonisin (corn flour). ~200 genes required for neurulation.
5.2.1 Anencephaly
Failure of anterior neuropore closure (day ≤24); ~50% of all open NTDs. Absence of calvaria; area cerebrovasculosa. Most stillborn; liveborns live <1–2 months maximum. Lethal condition
5.2.2 Myelomeningocele (MMC)
Failure of posterior neuropore closure (day ≤26); herniation of meninges + spinal cord. Most common: lumbar/lumbosacral region
Almost always + Chiari II + obstructive hydrocephalus. Hydrocephalus: 60% thoracic/sacral levels vs 90% thoracolumbar/lumbar
Management: closure within 48 hours (prevent infection); multidisciplinary (neurosurgery, urology, PT). Long-term: neurogenic bladder/bowel management essential

Fetal surgery (MOMS trial): prenatal repair → ↓ shunt placement + possibly better motor function + ↑ quality of life; but ↑ preterm delivery + maternal risks. ACOG: balance risks/benefits. No improvement in neurogenic bowel/bladder
5.2.3 Encephalocele
80% occipital in Western populations; frontal more common in Southeast Asia (Thailand!). Postneurulation disorder. About 50% have other major congenital anomalies
Outcomes: outcome depends on amount of neural tissue in defect; anterior defects better than posterior (0% vs 45% mortality in some series)
Evaluation: MRI with MR angiography/venography for surgical planning
6. Quick Reference Table — Brain Malformations
7. NICU Practical — Approach to Brain Malformation
Step 1 — Recognize: prenatal US/MRI → consult before birth; postnatally → seizures, hypotonia, abnormal OFC, abnormal facies → คิดถึง malformation
Step 2 — Image: cranial US ดีสำหรับ gross lesions; MRI is gold standard — better cortical detail, posterior fossa, white matter
Step 3 — Genetic workup: chromosome microarray (most yield); exome/genome sequencing ถ้า microarray negative; consider trio sequencing (parents + proband)
Step 4 — Subspecialty referrals: neurology (seizures), genetics, ophthalmology (optic hypoplasia, CMV), endocrinology (HPE/SOD pituitary), neurosurgery (hydrocephalus, MMC), urology (MMC)
Step 5 — Monitor complications: hydrocephalus (serial US/OFC), endocrinopathy (HPE/SOD — adrenal crisis/hypoglycemia!), seizures (EEG), feeding (OT/ST)
HPE specifically: DI ใน 70% → watch sodium + urine output closely; hypothyroidism; GH deficiency; VP shunt ถ้า hydrocephalus; tube feeding common
MMC specifically: protect exposed cord (moist sterile dressing, prone/side-lying); closure ≤48 hours; assess lower extremity motor/sensory; Chiari symptoms post-closure (apnea, stridor, poor feeding)
8. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — "The Face Predicts the Brain" ใน HPE
DeMyer 1964: severity of facial anomaly correlates with severity of HPE — แต่ไม่ absolute. Alobar HPE อาจมี normal facies; lobar/semilobar อาจมี severe facial anomaly. Midline anomalies: single central incisor, hypotelorism, midface hypoplasia = "microforms" ของ HPE (incomplete penetrance). Always image brain เมื่อมี midline facial anomaly.

PEARL 2 — Molar Tooth Sign = Joubert = ต้อง Monitor Kidney + Eye ตลอดชีวิต
Joubert syndrome เป็น ciliopathy — retinal dystrophy (progressive vision loss) + fibrocystic kidney disease + liver fibrosis ใน subset ของผู้ป่วย. Gene subtype สำคัญ: CEP290 → retinal dystrophy risk สูง. ดังนั้น genetic testing = mandatory ใน Joubert เพื่อ guide monitoring frequency. Recurrence risk = 25% → family counseling.

PEARL 3 — SOD + Pituitary = Adrenal Crisis Risk
SOD: hypopituitarism ใน up to 80%, with GH deficiency most common. แต่ที่อันตรายที่สุด = ACTH deficiency → adrenal insufficiency → adrenal crisis (hypoglycemia, hypotension, shock). Pituitary insufficiency can develop over time — ดังนั้น single normal screen ไม่เพียงพอ. Lifelong endocrine follow-up required.

PEARL 4 — Folate ป้องกัน 60–70% ของ NTD แต่ไม่ใช่ 100%
Folate periconceptional ลด NTD 60–70% — Thailand มี national fortification program. แต่ยังมี NTDs ที่ prevent ไม่ได้: chromosomal (trisomy 13/18), valproate (high risk แม้ folate), single gene disorders (Waardenburg). ดังนั้น unexplained NTD → genetic workup. Multiple affected pregnancies = higher recurrence than general population even with folate.

PEARL 5 — Bilateral Perisylvian PMG = Look for CMV
Bilateral perisylvian PMG คือ most common PMG pattern; etiologies หลายอย่าง แต่ CMV infection เป็น important acquired cause — especially ถ้า combined with periventricular calcifications. Always send CMV PCR urine/blood/saliva ใน neonates กับ bilateral PMG. CMV + PMG → sensorineural hearing loss risk สูง → audiologic assessment ทันที + consider ganciclovir.
9. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
DeMyer W et al. Pediatrics 1964;34:256: "The face predicts the brain" — classic paper establishing correlation between facial anomaly severity and HPE severity.

Adzick NS et al. NEJM 2011;364:993 (MOMS Trial): Prenatal vs postnatal MMC repair — prenatal surgery: ↓ shunt placement, possibly better motor; but ↑ preterm delivery and maternal risks. No improvement in neurogenic bowel/bladder.
Sotiriadis A & Makrydimas G. Am J Obstet Gynecol 2012;206:337: Systematic review ACC outcomes — 75% developing typically at early school age; 11% severe disability; isolated ACC better prognosis.

Bachmann-Gagescu R et al. J Med Genet 2015;52:514: Joubert syndrome — pathogenic variants in >35 genes; recessive; subsets with retinal dystrophy, kidney disease, liver fibrosis requiring specific monitoring by gene.
Aldinger KA et al. Am J Hum Genet 2019;105:606: Redefining etiologic landscape of cerebellar malformations — exome sequencing identified mutations in 16% DWM patients without known diagnosis.
Glass HC et al. Am J Med Genet 2008;146A:2495: Agenesis corpus callosum — chromosomal abnormality in 17% (aneuploidies 13, 18, 21); CNV overlap with autism.
Czeizel AE & Dudas I. NEJM 1992;327:1832: Periconceptional folate supplementation prevents first occurrence of NTDs — landmark RCT establishing folate as prevention.
Mlakar J et al. NEJM 2016;374:951: Zika virus associated with microcephaly — histopathology confirming ZIKV infects neural progenitor cells.
Roessler E et al. Nat Genet 1996;14:357: SHH mutations cause HPE — SHH accounts for 40% of familial HPE cases; sonic hedgehog signaling pathway central to forebrain development.
10. Self-Check Questions
Q: อธิบาย HPE 4 subtypes และ genetics — gene ไหนสำคัญที่สุด?

ตอบ: 4 subtypes: (1) alobar (complete; cyclopia/proboscis possible); (2) semilobar (>50% frontal fused, posterior partly separated); (3) lobar (near-complete fissure, absent CSP, rudimentary frontal horns); (4) MIH variant (middle interhemispheric — posterior frontal/parietal fails). Genetics: chromosomal 25–50% (trisomy 13, 18 most common); SHH = most common (40% familial); ZIC2 = most common de novo (high penetrance + characteristic face); others: SIX3, TGIF, GLI2. Maternal DM → 1–2% risk.
Q: Neonate กับ ACC — ต้อง workup อะไร? outcomes?

ตอบ: Workup: (1) chromosome microarray (17% associated anomaly); (2) brain MRI shortly after birth; (3) ophthalmologic evaluation; (4) genetics; (5) ND assessment; (6) pituitary function screen; (7) audiology. Outcomes: 75% developing typically at school age; 11% severe disability (isolated ACC). Worse prognosis if associated microcephaly, epilepsy, CP, cerebral dysgenesis. Specific deficits: abstract reasoning, linguistic pragmatics.
Q: Joubert syndrome: imaging findings + complications ที่ต้อง monitor?

ตอบ: Imaging: Molar Tooth Sign = cerebellar vermis hypoplasia + deep interpeduncular fossa + thick elongated superior cerebellar peduncles (axial MRI). Complications to monitor: retinal dystrophy (esp CEP290), fibrocystic kidney disease, liver fibrosis (all require lifelong monitoring + frequency by gene subtype). Recurrence 25% (AR). Genetic testing mandatory. Early features: apnea/tachypnea infancy, hypotonia, oculomotor abnormalities, head titubation.
Q: อธิบาย Walker-Warburg syndrome — ทำไมเรียก cobblestone cortex?

ตอบ: Walker-Warburg = most severe cobblestone cortex (type 2 lissencephaly). Cobblestone = disruption of pial surface → neurons overmigrate beyond cortex → nodular appearance. Cause: dystroglycanopathies (POMT1, POMT2, etc.) — abnormal O-glycosylation of alpha-dystroglycan. Features: macrocephaly (hydrocephalus), kinked brainstem, cerebellar hypoplasia, ↑ CK, retinal detachment, microphthalmia, severe muscular weakness. Survival beyond 1 year uncommon.
Q: MMC management ที่ NICU: ต้องทำอะไรก่อน 48 ชั่วโมง?

ตอบ: (1) Protect exposed cord: moist sterile non-stick dressing, prone/side-lying position (ไม่ pressure บน sac); (2) Closure within 48 hours เพื่อป้องกัน infection + optimize ND; (3) assess lower extremity sensory/motor, voiding/stooling pattern; (4) evaluate for other malformations (CHD, unless excluded prenatally); (5) head US / neurosurgical referral (Chiari II + hydrocephalus likely); (6) post-closure: monitor Chiari symptoms (apnea, stridor, poor feeding); (7) urology referral for neurogenic bladder (clean intermittent catheterization training).
Day 54 Preview

Avery Chapter 54 — Brain Injury in the Preterm Infant
พรุ่งนี้: brain ที่กำลัง develop (Ch52) ถูก injure ใน preterm — ผลที่ตามมา. เนื้อหาหลัก: IVH (Papile + Volpe grading), PVHI; WMI spectrum (cystic PVL → punctate → diffuse non-necrotic); preOL dysmaturation vs cell death; gray matter dysmaturation (↓ dendritic complexity without neuronal loss); Epo PENUT trial (negative!) + emerging neuroprotection; posthemorrhagic ventricular dilatation management. เชื่อมกับวันนี้: Ch53 covered structural malformations; Ch54 covers acquired injury ใน the actively developing preterm brain.
